stdout:
The patch introduces nondeterministic scaffold output for namespace-registry schemas, which can dirty generated validation files across equivalent init runs. I also observed workspace tests failing in this environment, but the actionable introduced issue is the unstable namespace ordering.

Review comment:

- [P2] Sort registry namespaces before serializing schema — /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/defaults.rs:111-111
  When `jit init` scaffolds defaults for any repo with more than one namespace, this collects `HashMap` keys in nondeterministic order and embeds that order in the `default:namespace-registry` regex inside `.jit/schemas/default-namespace-registry.json`. Because `HashMap` iteration order varies across runs/processes, the scaffolded schema can change without any config change, causing spurious git diffs and breaking the deterministic fresh-init/parity expectation. Sort `registered` before building the schema, as is already done for hierarchy types and uniqueness rules.
